currentFAQs.q: How much does new siding cost in {{CITY}}?
currentFAQs.a: Most homeowners in {{CITY}} pay between {{AVG_LOW}} and {{AVG_HIGH}} for siding replacement, depending on material and home size.

currentFAQs.q: Is fiber cement siding worth the extra cost?
currentFAQs.a: Fiber cement (like James Hardie) costs more than vinyl but lasts 30-50 years, resists fire and termites, and adds more resale value. It is a popular mid-range upgrade.

currentFAQs.q: How long does siding replacement take?
currentFAQs.a: Most siding projects take 1-2 weeks depending on home size and material. Vinyl installs fastest, while fiber cement and wood take longer.

proposedFAQs.note: Climate-driven: termite zones favor fiber cement; salt-air coastal favors fiber cement or vinyl; freeze-thaw markets favor engineered wood.
